message:
chore(artifacts): add parity_gate.yaml for phase2c packets 172-414

Extends the tracked phase2c parity-gate corpus from FP-P2D-171 up
through FP-P2D-414, adding 243 new parity_gate.yaml files — one per
packet directory. Gate configuration is deliberately strict across the
whole set and matches the pattern already established for packets
014-171:

  strict:
    critical_drift_budget: 0
    non_critical_drift_budget_percent: 0.1
  hardened:
    divergence_budget_percent: 1.0
    allowlisted_divergence_categories: null
  machine_check:
    suite: phase2c_packets
    require_fixture_count_at_least: 1 or 2
    require_failed: 0

Only parity_gate.yaml is tracked; the sibling parity_report.json,
parity_gate_result.json, parity_mismatch_corpus.json,
parity_report.decode_proof.json, and parity_report.raptorq.json outputs
remain gitignored (artifacts/phase2c/**/parity_*.json entries in
.gitignore lines 44-48) because they are regenerated on every harness
run and should not be versioned.

require_fixture_count_at_least is 2 for packets that ship two fixtures
(e.g. a strict + hardened pair) and 1 for single-fixture packets; the
value reflects the intended harness expectation, not the currently-
observed fixture count.

No code behavior change — these are pure configuration files consumed
by the phase2c_packets parity gate runner. The gate results for these
packets will continue to be "failed: ... but gate requires 0" until
their underlying oracle fixtures land, which matches the existing
pattern for packets 169-171.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
